- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F29475297&title=3.%20State%20of%20Mind%3A%20Level%204%20%7C%20The%20Mystery%20School&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=0166e32d-cc6c-4a1c-b85e-97ed6eff4d1c0dcfe4&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

## attachments

- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/e8dd3cc86d96459f84bc8205f69eb85d.jpeg](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/e8dd3cc86d96459f84bc8205f69eb85d.jpeg)

---

##    3\. State of Mind: Level 4 

Ok so, is everything going as fast as you want it to go?

Or do you still need to get your "state of mind" right?

Here are the levels in ascending order (I'll be going in detail in upcoming modules):

# **State of mind: Level 1.**

Narrowed goals. I know your ego wants you to be a musician / painter / poet / director. And you WILL be all of those things one day. But for now, become GREAT at one thing. Build a strong audience with it. THEN switch.

# **State of mind: Level 2**

Focused.

Your phone? The enemy. Bingeing shows? The enemy. The current news cycle? The enemy.

Time to focus.

# **State of mind: Level 3**

Consistency is queen. Only when you're focused can you be consistent.

Drop bombs more often.

But there is one state of mind that I hardly get to talk about.

Because most people are still trying climb the first 3.

It's my favorite one. 

Here it is if you're ready for it.

# **State of Mind: Level 4**

It's tempting to separate our lives into neat little boxes.

Creative time. Time to hang out with friends. Work. Alone time etc.

But the reality is...

If you're creative, that creativity is always bubbling under and waiting to be expressed. 

Creativity is a perpetual motion machine.

That's why you get amazing ideas at the weirdest times.

It's always below the surface. Working away...

But armed with this knowledge, there are two things you can do to maximize more frequent appearances of the muse in your life.

One of them you're already doing.

You learned it in the last module:

# **1\. Daily Practice (which gives you the "Zeigarnik Effect" that everyone wants).**

But the other way to unleash your creativity's full power is to get rid of the boxes.

# **2\. Start looking at every moment you've scheduled in your day as a part of one continuous creative flow.**

It may not feel natural at first. And some moments will be downright awkward.

"Father Bronques... You want me to see even my fights with my boyfriend as part of my creative flow? And even when I'm going for a shit? Like, how far are we taking this here?"

All the way.

If you want to improve your artistic output quality, change your relationship to everything you used to call mundane and undesirable.

Obsessive?

Maybe.

Observe your heroes. Read their biographies. Watch their documentaries. How did they behave on their ascent?

Imagine how much energy you'd have if you stopped judging everything.

Imagine how your mind would relax when you stop being at war with your schedule. And started seeing everything as connected instead.

Some of it is "maintenance". Some are activities you can't delegate to an assistant. Like visiting your grandmother... Or eating... Or going to the gym.

Activities you'll say are "stealing time away from your art"?

When the truth is...

What you do when you're NOT being creative has more influence on your art than anything else.

So why not treat it with that kind of respect? 

People have it half right when they say stuff like, "I need to 'go live a little' so I have something to say in my art."

But where they have it wrong is that they think they need to travel or _suffer trauma_. No, no. Chill out. 

You're experiencing things every day.

You just haven't been paying attention to the nuances.

Remember that funny scene in the Ray Charles movie?

The one where he's is fighting with his girl about "keeping the baby"? And she starts crying. And he's yelling. But suddenly, he realizes that this moment contains a bottled essence of great emotion... So he starts singing...

"Hit The Road Jack..."

And he starts playing the piano and writes the song.

Even she starts singing! Mad as hell and crying, she STILL understands:

You can't stop creativity.

So yeah, I DO want you to see your "fights with your boyfriend" as part of your creative flow from now on.

That and everything else.

(I remember a student taking two weeks off because their relationship was on the rocks. What?)

So let's imagine that from now on, activities like going to the gym will no longer be called "going to the gym"...

Rename it. Call it something like "exercising mental fortitude for my next deep dive in my writing".

Give care and attention to activities like flossing your teeth and doing your taxes.

The reward?

More energy in the totality of your existence. More flow. More art.

You will become a perpetual motion machine.

If you've never considered this, it may be uncomfortable to hear the first time around.

Maybe it challenges your whole belief system.

If so... Ignore it for now. 

Just make a mental note for later when you're at an important crossroad soul-searching.

[Continue here to the next module if you like... ](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21444354)

But...

If you've had similar thoughts already...

And all you needed was validation, or "permission", or a sign to dive right in... 

<sounds of angels and trumpets>

The old way is over.

"Leading an uninspired day and expecting great art to emerge" is a construct of the past. 

That used to work just fine:

You wrestled with your mind-- and eventually art came out. 

But I want you to be more than "fine" for you.

"Imagine what it would look like if it were easy."

**There's a whole new world waiting to show up at your creative doorstep when you start looking at the entire picture of your life as ONE LONG FLOW ACTIVITY.**

Are you ready to adopt this? Start right after you read this. You'll feel the change immediately. So will everyone around you.

It's that powerful.

Inject creativity in every "in-between" moment of your day.

No matter how awkward.

Even if the normals call you "eccentric".

And let your language reflect the changes. Other Level 4 creatives will understand you right away.

_(These sentences are all taken from real life. From famous people who's work you consume. From people I know you admire. So shhhhhh.):_

"Everything I do, I do it for art. There's no point in the day where I'm not trying to sneak it in there."

"I only sleep so I have more energy and ideas. And the only reason I eat is so I don't crash when all my best ideas start coming."

"I wake up at 4 am. I drink water, but I don't eat. I meditate. I know it doesn't sound creative. But without it, I'm useless."

"If I didn't walk my dogs every morning, I wouldn't have written (hit song)."

_You get the idea._

Now, how about you?

_What "crazy" things will come out of your mouth when you start living every moment like they're scenes from a movie?  _

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

"start living every moment like they're scenes from a movie."... wow, this had the idea of the 4th state of mind click like a clapperboard before shooting a scene. It's a mindset! To simultaneously act and be the director, becoming aware of the underlying message of the scene you're living in the present. Beautiful idea as always. Hereby, I sign the contract. Permission granted. Scene 25, Take 12, Action

Yeah I don’t make art because I feel so uninspired because I have no social life, no job, no car to explore the city, I don’t live in a “cool neighborhood”. I’m always bored to death so I scroll on Ig and smoke weed. I keep saying that if I had things to do I would be more “inspired” but deep down I know it would be the same. So yes, I needed this. I’m really glad I found TMS

![Lisa Wilson](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/9064df82a8b4a214503d002e12af3e2b?d=mm&s=70)

Lisa Wilson

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/29475297/comments/19800713)

My most creative time ever was when my ex and I split in summer of 2021. That breakup stripped me of all of my possessions, including my home. I was homeless from August - December and crashed friends couches, slept in offices, Airbnbs, hotels while finishing grad school. Somehow I found the energy to go to the dance studio to train to get through that pain. I utilized my gym to crosstrain daily. I came up with my best choreography in those months and leveled up as an athlete. I was in the flow in spite of what i was going through. The energy I got in finding my creativity in dance again was directly related to admitting my dignity mattered more than my ex's opinion of what I should do with my career and life. Then, in 2022, i got comfortable in my own apartment, started paying massive bills, and began to sit on my ass all day applying to corporate jobs in design. I am still trying to figure out what all of this means but I know I lost my flow and I'm split so many ways, missing doing the thing I love that doesn't pay enough, putting time into applying for design jobs so that i can get steady paid, and dancing at clubs to stay afloat in between. I'm split and exhausted. The creativity for dance is dry. I am nowhere near being creative as a designer as the kind of design I do is research design and it is the most boring shit ever. I miss dancing but it seems like I made a choice to get into design so I can get financial stability. I dream of having my own studio someday so I can dance again. I miss that flow.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

You should do the thing you love first every day. Before you give your sweat and energy to your day job. That's how you stay aligned and find reserves you never knew you had. But you'll only hear what I just said when your fire to create is burning hot. Until then, it'll be a wish, but not "do or die".

Man!!! Now this just leveled me up. You do the challenge and then you're like "now what?" Like you can't stop being productive but I'd judge myself in the in-between times and notice the areas where my energy got low from not creating (driving, eating, etc). But injecting creativity into everything??? Hell yes. I'm recharged and I didn't even know I needed to be.

It's funny because when you think about life on a spiritual level, everything truly is connected and it really is one continuous flow of energy and activity. Everything. All the time. Never stopping. It's not even like some crazy artist thing. It's the very foundation of existence as we know and experience it here in this realm. So it only makes sense to put it all together and portray it in a way that only you have experienced it.

![RT Herwig](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/254f48f87ea53593b3ab8b035c17b8e6?d=mm&s=70)

RT Herwig

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/29475297/comments/11213019)

Ok. Ok. I think this is the place that I need to pull apart a little. By day, I'm an advertising Creative Director that needs to push creativity, vision, and high energy all day. I'd rather be putting this time and energy into my own work. I totally understand the obsessive thing for both film and advertising creativity. In fact, that's how I was able to find accomplishments in both. I have one REAL feature film under my belt that played festivals and was picked up for distribution, but then I traded in that for a 9-5 career in advertising because we were having a baby. I thought I could balance the two, but the need for a bigger salary and position grew and grew. While I have written a few scripts and am in the middle of developing what I hoped would be my next feature, it is so hard to figure out what to focus on. Ads or Films. Which one do I turbo boost into Level 4? I can flip and flop on that choice daily. Knowing that I don't want to live a life of regret I sway towards directing, but the responsible side always says, you can do all this same thinking and traverse these states of mind for your day job. This is the crazy hamster wheel I am on. And what I am looking for is that key that will crack the answer open. It's true I could do both and do both well. BUT.... the angels and trumpets sound when I focus on film. I am talking gibberish. I'm just saying it's hard to stay high level trying to do both. I feel like I am going to crack. I probably already have. I dunno. Onward towards Level 4.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

Tim back to this and it helped a lot, especially the state of mind peace. I have A bunch of projects and clients that are coming up, that will take a lot of green space and time, and I’m nervous about the amount of energy/feelings working with them all that’s going to bring up for me, especially because none of it is related to my art. But of course… It all is!
